note: seq=55 (ASYNC ENRICH CANARY -- self-diagnosing gate). Deploys atlas_enrich_async.py as the additive Hetzner-only atlas-enrich-async@1 (32 lanes/8 pool, MemoryMax=2G), live fleet untouched. The first attempt failed the bare --selftest on the box (passed in py3.10 sandbox), so this revision runs /opt/atlas/async_canary_gate.py: it executes --selftest CAPTURING the box python version + output to status/hetzner/async-canary.json, starts the canary ONLY if selftest passes, and ALWAYS exits 0 so a selftest failure can't block the queue. Bounded PG pool (<=8 conns) regardless of lane count; same enrichment logic as the live worker. After it lands I read async-canary.json to confirm pass+start (or see the exact error) and then measure done-rate/RSS/pg_stat_activity. Allowlist-safe; no stop/disable; live fleet untouched.

steps.note: seq=55: async canary with self-diagnosing gate (never blocks the queue).

steps.cmd: /opt/atlas/venv/bin/python -c "import hashlib,sys; w={'/opt/atlas/importers/atlas_enrich_async.py':'9a58ee77db5dbbd82d87d1bdecdd96694f57cba81ce97fef217754904222a7b1','/opt/atlas/async_canary_gate.py':'412105fb269d79cb62cc8d6dd11230b33650ba0ae3ebfcb21e44d2027536188a'}; [sys.exit('SHA MISMATCH '+p) for p,h in w.items() if hashlib.sha256(open(p,'rb').read()).hexdigest()!=h]; print('sha ok')"

steps.content:
[Unit]
Description=ATLAS async enrichment engine CANARY (asyncio; bounded PG pool)
After=network-online.target postgresql.service
Wants=network-online.target

[Service]
Type=simple
Nice=10
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/db.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/autopull.env
EnvironmentFile=-/etc/atlas/async.env
Environment=ATLAS_ASYNC_LANES=32
Environment=ATLAS_ASYNC_PG_POOL=8
Environment=ATLAS_ASYNC_DB_WRITERS=4
ExecStart=/opt/atlas/venv/bin/python /opt/atlas/importers/atlas_enrich_async.py
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=10
MemoryHigh=1500M
MemoryMax=2G

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
